text.title: Quality Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the quality-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Lock DoD per repo in quality/dod.yaml and version it — agents and CI read the file, not human prose.
rule.rationale: Human-prose DoD drifts within weeks; machine-readable file enforced by CI makes 'done' unambiguous and diff-auditable.

rule.statement: Separate must items from should items in DoD; only must items block merge — should items open a follow-up issue automatically.
rule.rationale: A DoD that blocks every item becomes a bypass incentive under pressure; the split preserves coverage and velocity.

rule.statement: Run quality gates twice: pre-commit (fast — lint, format) and pre-merge (full — tests, coverage, security scan). Do not duplicate slow checks.
rule.rationale: Duplicating slow checks kills developer velocity; checking only late creates surprises. The split is the correct trade-off.

rule.statement: Track defect escape rate (bugs found in production / total bugs) as a leading indicator; apply SPC charting to distinguish signal from noise.
rule.rationale: A single defect count is a lagging snapshot; escape rate trend exposes whether the quality system is improving or degrading.

rule.statement: For agent-written code, require 90%+ coverage, mutation testing on critical modules, no warning suppressions without explanatory comment.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated code requires higher scrutiny; mutation testing reveals tautological tests that pad coverage.

rule.statement: N/a tags on DoD items require explicit human approval; the agent cannot auto-skip.
rule.rationale: N/a bypass is the most exploited DoD escape hatch.

list.item: One-person prototype before product-market fit — quality gates slow validation
Spike or research code marked throwaway — formal QC inflates effort 2-3x
When the team rejects DoD as ceremony — fix the trust issue first
Aesthetic UX polish — use design review, not quality management process
